metadata.title: Tenant Context Propagation to Async Event Processors

story.iWant: tenant context propagated to async Axon event processors
story.soThat: projection updates and event handlers have tenant context available
story.tasks: - AC1: AxonTenantInterceptor.kt implements EventMessageHandlerInterceptor - AC2: Interceptor extracts tenant_id from event metadata - AC3: TenantContext.set(tenantId) before event handler execution - AC4: Context cleared after handler completion - AC5: Event metadata enriched with tenant_id during command processing - AC6: Integration test validates: dispatch command → event handler has tenant context - AC7: Async event processors (TrackingEventProcessor) receive correct context

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. AxonTenantInterceptor.kt implements EventMessageHandlerInterceptor 2. Interceptor extracts tenant_id from event metadata 3. TenantContext.set(tenantId) before event handler execution 4. Context cleared after handler completion 5. Event metadata enriched with tenant_id during command processing 6. Integration test validates: dispatch command → event handler has tenant context 7. Async event processors (TrackingEventProcessor) receive correct context

doc.section: Section 16 - Context Propagation to Async Processors
doc.relevance: Async event processors run on different threads, require context propagation via metadata
doc.snippet: Async Context Propagation for Multi-Tenancy: - Async event processors (TrackingEventProcessor) run on separate threads - ThreadLocal tenant context does NOT propagate automatically to async threads - Solution: Enrich event metadata with tenant_id during command processing - EventMessageHandlerInterceptor extracts tenant_id from metadata, restores to ThreadLocal - Context cleanup after event handler execution prevents leaks - Critical for: projection updates, saga event handlers, async domain event handlers

doc.section: Story 4.0 Tasks - Axon ThreadLocal Propagation Patterns
doc.relevance: Epic 3 retrospective identified need for Axon ThreadLocal propagation research
doc.snippet: Story 4.0 Task: Research Axon ThreadLocal Propagation Patterns (Charlie, ~4h) - Investigate MessageHandlerInterceptor for context propagation - Prototype ThreadLocal → async event processor pattern - Document in architecture.md "Multi-Tenancy" section - Prevents cross-tenant leaks in Story 4.5 Epic 2/3 Pattern: mode=subscribing for test event processors

doc.relevance: Axon interceptor patterns and async event processing
doc.snippet: EventMessageHandlerInterceptor pattern for async event processors. Interceptor runs BEFORE event handler on async processor thread. UnitOfWork provides event processing context (metadata access). MetaData.get(key) extracts custom metadata from events. TrackingEventProcessor uses token store for async event replay.

doc.relevance: Business requirement for tenant context in async processing
doc.snippet: Async event processors must maintain tenant isolation. Projection updates require tenant context for RLS (Layer 3). Event handlers must not leak context between tenants.

Story 4.5: Tenant Context Propagation to Async Event Processors - AxonTenantInterceptor.kt implements EventMessageHandlerInterceptor - Interceptor extracts tenant_id from event metadata - TenantContext.set(tenantId) before event handler execution - Context cleared after handler completion - Event metadata enriched with tenant_id during command processing

artifact.reason: Story 4.3 CommandHandlerInterceptor pattern applies to EventMessageHandlerInterceptor
Widget projection event handlers to receive tenant context
Axon configuration for registering event interceptors
Widget projection integration tests with async event processing

dependency.name: Testcontainers
dependency.purpose: Integration tests with PostgreSQL, Axon Server

constraints.constraint: Async event processors run on different threads than command handlers. ThreadLocal does NOT automatically propagate to async threads. Must explicitly extract from event metadata and restore to ThreadLocal.
Enrich event metadata with tenant_id DURING command processing. Use Axon MetaData.with("tenant_id", tenantId) when applying events. Metadata propagates with event to async processors.
Clear TenantContext after event handler completes (try-finally pattern). Prevents context leaks in async processor thread pool. Cleanup critical for TrackingEventProcessor with long-running threads.

Missing tenant_id in event metadata → log warning, continue processing. Async processors may handle non-tenant events (system events). Do NOT fail-closed for async processors (different failure mode than commands).
TrackingEventProcessor uses token store for async event replay. Context must be restored correctly during replay scenarios. Test replay with tenant context propagation.

interface.name: Axon EventMessageHandlerInterceptor
interface.description: Axon interceptor for async event handlers. Extracts tenant_id from event metadata, restores to ThreadLocal.

interface.name: Event Metadata Enrichment
interface.description: Enrich event metadata with tenant_id during command processing. Metadata propagates to async event handlers.

interface.name: Async Context Validation
interface.description: Validate tenant context is available in async event handlers. Use for projection updates requiring tenant context.

standards.standard: Constitutional TDD: Integration test BEFORE interceptor implementation. Test validates: dispatch command → event handler has tenant context.
Use mode=subscribing for test event processors (Epic 2/3 pattern). Validate async TrackingEventProcessor receives tenant context. Test event replay scenarios with context propagation.

Test scenarios: - Tenant A command → Tenant A event handler (correct context) - Concurrent tenant A + B commands → isolated event handlers - Event without tenant_id metadata → handler continues (log warning) - TrackingEventProcessor replay → context restored correctly

ideas.idea: Test AC2: Extract tenant_id from event metadata in interceptor
Test AC3: Verify TenantContext.getCurrentTenantId() returns correct tenant in event handler
Test AC4: Context cleared after event handler completes (ThreadLocal cleanup)
Test AC5: Event metadata enriched with tenant_id during command processing
Test AC6: Dispatch CreateWidgetCommand → WidgetCreatedEvent handler has tenant context
Test AC7: TrackingEventProcessor receives tenant context for async event
Test concurrent multi-tenant events → isolated context per event
Test event without tenant_id metadata → handler continues, logs warning
Test event replay scenario → context restored correctly from metadata
Test projection update with tenant context → RLS Layer 3 enforces isolation
